checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully captures the text present in the source image ('Wear a Mask', 'We appreciate your cooperation', 'DON’T WAIT TO VACCINATE!', 'Visit MySpot.nc.gov to find a vaccine location near you', and 'NC DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES'). There is no text in the JSON that does not appear in the source. The title and section heading are derived directly from the primary text of the flyer.

The source document contains one URL: 'MySpot.nc.gov'. The output JSON correctly captures this URL and provides a valid hyperlink object (including the standard https protocol). No phone numbers or emails exist in the source to be checked.

The source document consists of a single page. The JSON output contains metadata indicating a page count of 1 and includes all primary text sections found on that page. While the visual elements (mask illustration and state seal) are not explicitly present as image objects in the JSON, the informational content of the page is fully represented.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All major text sections from the source are present in the output. The only omitted text is the highly decorative text within the state seal ('THE GREAT SEAL of the STATE of NORTH CAROLINA', etc.), which is typically considered part of a graphic element rather than body text. This omission is minor and does not constitute a significant portion (25%) of the document's text content.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the JSON output exactly matches the source document. Normalization such as using a standard apostrophe and formatting the call-to-action as a heading (Level 2) are acceptable transformations for accessibility and structure.

checks.a_analysis: The output content aligns with the source document. Each text block ('Wear a Mask', 'We appreciate your cooperation.', etc.) appears exactly once in the JSON, matching the source layout.

The main title 'Wear a Mask' is used as the section title (implied H1). The subsequent prominent text in the blue box 'DON’T WAIT TO VACCINATE!' is correctly tagged as a level 2 heading. There are no skipped levels or logical inconsistencies in the hierarchy.
The source document contains no tables; accordingly, the output contains no table structures.
The JSON preserves the top-to-bottom reading order of the original poster: title, appreciative statement, vaccination call-to-action, and footer agency information.
The source document contains no lists; accordingly, the output contains no list elements.
The URL 'MySpot.nc.gov' present in the source is correctly captured as a functional link in the JSON with the appropriate 'https://' prefix.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the vertical reading order of the source document. While the styling is simplified (losing the blue color theme, the rounded container box, and specific font sizes), the information organization matches. A user can find the same textual information in the same relative order. The conversion to a single-column accessible layout is expected and not a defect.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, there are no tables to misrender in the output.

The source PDF contains two significant images: a central illustration of a surgical mask and the North Carolina State Seal logo in the footer next to the department name. Neither of these images appears in the HTML output screenshot. While the department name text is present, the seal logo and the primary visual illustration (the mask) are missing. Per the instructions, meaningful images including logos should be present.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains a large blue mask illustration in the center and the NC State Seal logo in the bottom footer.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows only text; the mask illustration and the state seal logo are missing.

checks.a_analysis: There are no instances of duplicated body content. Each text element from the source ('Wear a Mask', 'We appreciate your cooperation', etc.) appears exactly once in the output body.
